title: Lesson 3 – Analysing Data and Big Data
description: Application of big data concepts, statistical measures, expected values and normal distribution in management decision making
subject: Management Accounting

questions.question: A retail company collects billions of customer transactions, website clicks and social media interactions daily. The finance team states that traditional spreadsheet tools cannot efficiently process this data. Which term best describes this situation?
questions.rationale: Big data refers to extremely large and complex datasets that cannot be effectively processed using traditional tools.

questions.question: A logistics company collects real time GPS data from thousands of delivery vehicles to optimize routes instantly. Which characteristic of big data does this mainly illustrate?
questions.rationale: Velocity refers to the speed at which data is generated and processed.

questions.question: A retailer analyses purchase transactions, product images, customer reviews and audio feedback to understand customer behaviour. Which big data characteristic is illustrated?
questions.rationale: Variety refers to the different types and formats of data used in big data analysis.

questions.question: A bank relies heavily on large customer datasets but discovers that some records contain errors and outdated information. Which big data characteristic is being questioned?
questions.rationale: Veracity relates to the accuracy and reliability of the data being used.

questions.question: A retailer analyses customer purchasing patterns to predict future demand and optimise stock levels. What is the primary benefit gained from this big data application?
questions.rationale: Big data analysis helps organisations optimise operations and improve efficiency.

questions.question: A streaming service analyses user viewing history to recommend movies and personalise content suggestions. Which advantage of big data is demonstrated?
questions.rationale: Big data enables organisations to personalise services based on customer behaviour.

questions.question: A company invests heavily in big data infrastructure but finds it difficult to recruit skilled analysts to interpret the data. Which risk of big data is illustrated?
questions.rationale: A lack of skilled professionals capable of analysing big data is a key risk associated with big data systems.

questions.question: A manufacturing firm analyses equipment sensor data to predict machine failures before they occur. Which big data benefit is primarily being used?
questions.rationale: Big data helps identify risks and patterns that enable preventative action.

questions.question: A company calculates the mean daily sales of a product over 10 days by dividing the total sales by 10. Which statistical measure is being used?
questions.rationale: The arithmetic mean is calculated by dividing the total of observations by the number of observations.

questions.question: The weekly sales figures for a product are arranged in ascending order. The manager selects the middle value to represent the typical sales level. Which measure is used?
questions.rationale: The median is the middle value when data is arranged in order.

questions.question: A company analyses production quantities and notices that the value 500 units appears more frequently than any other value. Which measure of average is represented?
questions.rationale: The mode is the value that occurs most frequently in a dataset.

questions.question: Two factories have identical mean output levels, but one factory has a much higher standard deviation. What does this indicate?
options: Output is more consistent in the second factory
Output varies more widely in the first factory
Both factories have identical production patterns
The first factory has a higher mean
questions.answer: Output varies more widely in the first factory
questions.rationale: Standard deviation measures how widely data values spread around the mean.

questions.question: A manager wants to compare variability between two datasets with very different means. Which measure is most appropriate?
questions.rationale: The coefficient of variation standardises variability relative to the mean.

questions.question: A decision maker estimates that a project has a 0.6 probability of generating a profit of 2000 and a 0.4 probability of generating a loss of 500. Which technique would evaluate the average expected outcome?
questions.rationale: Expected value calculates the weighted average outcome based on probabilities.

questions.question: A manager selects the option with the highest expected value when evaluating several investment alternatives. What assumption is being made?
options: The decision will be repeated many times
questions.answer: The decision will be repeated many times
questions.rationale: Expected value represents the long run average outcome if decisions are repeated many times.

questions.question: A firm bases its investment decision solely on expected value without considering the variability of possible outcomes. What limitation of expected value is illustrated?
questions.rationale: Expected value focuses on average outcomes and ignores variability or risk.

questions.question: A data distribution is perfectly symmetrical and forms a bell shaped curve. Which type of distribution does this represent?
questions.rationale: A normal distribution is symmetrical and forms a bell shaped curve.

questions.question: In a normal distribution, what is the relationship between the mean, median and mode?
questions.rationale: In a normal distribution the mean, median and mode coincide at the centre.

questions.question: A dataset follows a normal distribution with mean 50. What proportion of observations are expected to lie below the mean?
questions.rationale: In a symmetrical normal distribution half of the values lie below the mean.

questions.question: A manager converts a data value into a Z score when analysing a normal distribution. What is the purpose of this transformation?
options: To convert values to a standard normal distribution
To remove variability from the data
questions.answer: To convert values to a standard normal distribution
questions.rationale: The Z score converts values into the standard normal distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1.

questions.question: A technology firm implements Hadoop and Spark to process extremely large datasets. What organisational requirement is primarily being addressed?
options: The need for tools and infrastructure to process big data
The need to reduce transaction costs
The need to eliminate human data
The need to avoid statistical analysis
questions.answer: The need for tools and infrastructure to process big data
questions.rationale: Big data requires specialised tools and infrastructure for processing and analysis.

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristics of big data?
questions.rationale: Big data characteristics include volume, velocity, variety, veracity and value.

questions.question: Which of the following are benefits of using big data in organisations?
questions.rationale: Big data provides insights that improve decisions, customer experience and efficiency.

questions.question: Which of the following are limitations of expected value analysis?
options: Only represents a long term average
answer: Only represents a long term average
questions.rationale: Expected value depends on probabilities, represents long term averages and ignores risk.

questions.question: The statistical measure that represents the most frequently occurring value in a dataset is called the ________.
questions.rationale: The mode represents the value that occurs most frequently.

questions.question: The ________ measures the average spread of data values around the mean.
questions.rationale: Standard deviation measures how far values deviate from the mean.

questions.question: A symmetrical bell shaped probability distribution is known as the ________ distribution.
questions.rationale: The normal distribution is characterised by a symmetrical bell shaped curve.

questions.question: The [dropdown_1] of a dataset is calculated by dividing the total of all values by the number of observations, while the [dropdown_2] identifies the most frequently occurring value.
questions.rationale: Mean is the arithmetic average while mode is the most frequent value.

questions.question: The [dropdown_1] measures the spread of data around the mean, while the [dropdown_2] is its squared value.
questions.rationale: Variance is the square of the standard deviation.

questions.question: In a normal distribution the [dropdown_1], [dropdown_2] and mode occur at the centre of the distribution.
questions.rationale: In a normal distribution the mean, median and mode coincide.

questions.question: Match each big data characteristic with its meaning.

matchPairs.right: Speed at which data is generated

questions.question: Match each statistical measure with its description.

matchPairs.right: Middle value in an ordered dataset

questions.question: Match each concept with its meaning.

matchPairs.right: Average spread of values around the mean
Standard deviation divided by the mean

questions.question: Match each application with the industry example.
Match each concept with its description.

matchPairs.right: Weighted average outcome based on probabilities
Standardised value used with normal distribution
